hero.desc: At Nexus Clinic Kuala Lumpur, we build a scar map for your face and match it with the right mix of treatments, so your skin looks smoother in real life, not just in photos.
hero.honestQuote: "There is no single 'best acne scar treatment' for everyone. Acne scars come in different shapes, depths, and directions."

whyStubborn.title: Why acne scars feel so stubborn
whyStubborn.subtitle: (and why they are not your fault)
whyStubborn.desc: Acne scars are not just "marks on the surface." Many scars sit deeper because acne inflammation damages collagen below the skin. When healing happens unevenly, the surface can dip, tether, or look rough.
whyStubborn.goodNewsDesc: Scars can be improved. The skin can rebuild collagen. It just needs the right trigger and enough time.
whyStubborn.realityDesc: Even people who never pick can still scar, especially with cystic acne. But with proper treatment, meaningful improvement is achievable.

scarTypes.title: Types of Acne Scars
scarTypes.desc: Most facial acne scars fall into these patterns. Identifying your scar type is the first step to effective treatment.
scarTypes.type1Desc: Small, narrow, deep 'holes' that need targeted treatment approaches
scarTypes.type2Desc: Wider dents with clearer edges, like old chickenpox marks
scarTypes.type3Desc: Soft wave-like dents from bands of scar tissue pulling skin down
scarTypes.type4Desc: Hypertrophic or keloid scars needing different approaches
scarTypes.postAcneDesc: Brown or red marks after acne are very common in Malaysia. They can look like scarring, but they are usually pigment or redness. They often respond well to pigment-friendly plans and strict sun protection.

whoFor.goodFitTitle: You are a good fit if:
whoFor.fit1: Your acne is mostly under control and the scars are the main problem
whoFor.fit2: Your scars show more in certain lighting, selfies, or video calls
whoFor.fit3: You want improvement, not 'perfect glass skin'
whoFor.fit4: You can commit to a plan (usually multiple sessions)
whoFor.waitTitle: You may need to wait or adjust if:
whoFor.wait1: Active, inflamed acne that keeps flaring

scarMapping.title: The Nexus Scar Mapping Plan
scarMapping.desc: A good acne scar plan should feel clear, not confusing. Here's how we build yours.
scarMapping.step1Desc: We analyze scar types, depth, tethering, skin sensitivity, pigment risk, and your lifestyle
scarMapping.step2Desc: Often a combination approach - fractional CO2, subcision, microneedling, peels, PRP, and more
scarMapping.step3Desc: We discuss what 'good progress' looks like for your face and timeline

treatments.title: Acne Scar Treatments
treatments.desc: Explained in easy words - each with specific benefits for different scar types
treatments.t1Desc: Releases fibrotic strands tethering scars, allowing skin to lift naturally
treatments.t2Desc: Creates micro-zones for skin healing and new collagen building
treatments.t3Desc: Controlled micro-injuries that encourage collagen production
treatments.t4Desc: Platelet-rich plasma support for enhanced healing

benefits.con1: It takes time. Collagen does not rebuild overnight
benefits.con2: Downtime can be inconvenient if you choose resurfacing lasers
benefits.con3: You may need a combination plan for mixed scars

sideEffects.title: Side effects and safety
sideEffects.subtitle: what you should know in Malaysia
sideEffects.desc: Most acne scar procedures are safe when performed by trained medical professionals, using proper settings and aftercare. Malaysia has guidance around aesthetic medical practice and credentialing.
sideEffects.effect4: Temporary pigment changes (higher risk in darker skin)
sideEffects.safetyQuote: "The best safety move you can make is simple: follow aftercare, avoid sun, and do not 'over-treat' too fast."

cost.title: Cost of acne scar treatment
cost.subtitle: in Kuala Lumpur and Malaysia
cost.desc: Prices vary by scar severity, treatment type, device used, and number of sessions.
cost.note: At Nexus Clinic Kuala Lumpur, your cost depends on your scar map and chosen plan.

aftercare.title: Aftercare that protects your results
aftercare.desc: Your aftercare is not "optional." It is part of the treatment.
aftercare.betweenDesc: Do not rush. Your skin needs time to rebuild collagen. If acne returns, treat it early so scars do not keep forming.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.q1: How many sessions of fractional CO2 laser are needed?
faq.a1: Many patients are guided toward 3 to 5 sessions, often spaced 4 to 6 weeks apart, depending on scar depth and goals.
faq.q2: Is fractional CO2 laser safe for darker skin tones?
faq.a2: It can be safe with proper assessment and customised settings, but pigment risk needs planning. Darker skin tones may have a higher risk of pigmentation changes.
faq.q3: What is subcision and what scars is it for?
faq.a3: Subcision is a minor procedure where a needle is used to release tethering strands under the skin, helping depressed scars lift and encouraging collagen formation.
faq.q4: What is a realistic acne scar improvement goal?
faq.a4: Visible improvement in texture and depth. Many patients don't need 'perfect,' they want smoother skin in real lighting.

whyNexus.title: Why Nexus Clinic Kuala Lumpur
whyNexus.desc: If you are searching for acne scar treatment in Kuala Lumpur, you are probably overwhelmed by options. Many clinics offer lasers. Many offer microneedling. The difference is how well your plan is built.
whyNexus.point1: Scar mapping and combination planning for mixed scars
whyNexus.point3: Complete toolkit: subcision, CO2, dermaroller, peels, PRP
whyNexus.point4: Easy access at Wisma UOA II, Jalan Pinang
